lines: I am Caddoc Wick, keeper of the last lantern. If it burns low, Deep Forest pretends it never heard of roads.
Take the bell-note eastward: one ring for courage, two for courtesy, and none for hurrying.
The last lantern has heard your bell-note. Even the brambles must mind a polite traveler now.
Mind the oil crate. It leaks only when someone boasts about never slipping.
